title: Department of Water Working to Increase Water Pressure Overnight
summary: The City of Syracuse Department of Water is addressing low water pressure across multiple city areas caused by a combination of factors: supply constraints from Skaneateles Lake, two restarted eastside pumps following power outages, and depleted reservoir levels due to multiple water main breaks this year, including a major break on the north side today. Pressure improvements are anticipated overnight.

political_signal.description: Press release demonstrates Department of Water's operational responsiveness to infrastructure failure. Indicates baseline water system vulnerability and management of multiple concurrent failures (power outages, main breaks, supply constraints).

processing_notes: Author field listed as 'Unknown' in source document
Mayor Sharon F. Owens mentioned in header navigation but not in article body
Contact number provided: 315-448-8360 for Department of Water
Multiple causative factors suggest systemic infrastructure stress rather than isolated incident
No budget, timeline, or remediation cost information provided
Article timestamp indicates evening publication (9:45 PM) for overnight repair work
